title: Iliad, Book 22: The Death of Hector
description: Book 22 of Homer's Iliad: The Death of Hector.
introduction: Book 22 of the Iliad: The Death of Hector. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ὣς οἱ μὲν κατὰ ἄστυ πεφυζότες ἠΰτε νεβροί.
translation.text: The Trojans fled inside the walls like fawns. Hector alone remained outside. Priam and Hecuba begged him to come in, but he would not. Achilles chased him three times around the city. Athena tricked Hector with a phantom, and Achilles drove his spear through Hector's throat. Dying, Hector begged for his body to be returned. Achilles refused. He dragged the body behind his chariot.
